summary: Iteration 16: Simulation Builder frontend fully tested. Fixed critical drag-and-drop bug. All core features verified: 8 simulation types display, template loading, building blocks palette with 5 categories, drag-and-drop to canvas, live preview updates, Templates tab with pre-built templates, Saved tab showing 35 saved simulations.

simulation_builder_page_load.details: Page loads at /simulation-builder with title 'Simulation Builder - Create custom security simulations with drag-and-drop'

eight_simulation_types.details: All 8 types displayed: Phishing Email, Credential Harvest, QR Code Phishing, MFA Fatigue, USB Drop, SMS Phishing, Business Email Compromise, Malicious Ad

type_selection_shows_canvas.details: Clicking a simulation type shows building blocks palette, drop canvas, and live preview

building_blocks_palette.details: 5 categories: CONTENT (Header/Title, Email Subject, Sender Info, Body Text, Signature), FORM ELEMENTS (Input Field, Password Field, Submit Button, Checkbox), PHISHING TACTICS (Urgency Message, Deadline/Timer, Threat/Warning, CTA Button, Fake Link), VISUAL ELEMENTS (scrollable), SPECIAL ELEMENTS

quick_start_templates.details: MFA Fatigue has 'Okta MFA Bombardment' and 'Microsoft 365 MFA Attack'. Credential Harvest has 'Microsoft Login Page', 'Google Sign In', 'Bank Portal Login'

template_loading.details: Loading 'Okta MFA Bombardment' template adds 4 blocks to canvas, sets simulation name, updates live preview with Okta Verify content

drag_and_drop.details: Fixed bug where dragging blocks to canvas caused runtime error. Now works: toast shows 'Added Header/Title', block appears in canvas with input field, live preview updates

live_preview.details: Live Preview updates in real-time when template loaded. Shows header, form inputs, buttons, MFA prompts based on block content

templates_tab.details: Shows pre-built templates organized by type: MFA Fatigue Templates (2), USB Drop Templates (2), QR Code Phishing Templates (2), Credential Harvest Templates (3), Phishing Email Templates (2). Each has 'Use Template' button

use_template_from_templates_tab.details: Clicking 'Use Template' for Microsoft Login Page loads 6 blocks, switches to Builder tab, shows credential harvest form in live preview

saved_simulations_tab.details: Shows 35 saved simulations in grid view. Each shows type icon, title, type name, and difficulty badge (easy/medium/hard)

three_tabs_navigation.details: Builder, Templates, Saved (35) tabs all work correctly with proper navigation

simulation_name_input.details: Input field for simulation name visible when type selected. Auto-filled when template loaded

difficulty_selector.details: Dropdown with Easy/Medium/Hard options visible and functional

save_and_preview_buttons.details: Full Preview and Save Simulation buttons visible in header when type selected

change_type_button.details: Change Type button visible to go back to type selection

bugs_fixed.issue: Runtime error 'Element type is invalid' when dragging building blocks to canvas
bugs_fixed.root_cause: JSON.stringify loses React component functions. When block.icon (a React component) was serialized for drag-and-drop, it became an empty object {} instead of a function. Rendering this caused 'Element type is invalid' error.
bugs_fixed.fix: Modified handleDrop function to look up the original block by ID from BUILDING_BLOCKS array to restore the icon component after JSON deserialization

context_for_next_testing_agent: Simulation Builder fully functional. Fixed critical drag-and-drop bug in handleDrop function. All features working: 1) 8 simulation types (Phishing Email, Credential Harvest, QR Code Phishing, MFA Fatigue, USB Drop, SMS Phishing, BEC, Malicious Ad), 2) Building blocks with 5 categories (Content, Form Elements, Phishing Tactics, Visual Elements, Special Elements), 3) Template loading via Quick Start Templates and Templates tab, 4) Drag-and-drop blocks to canvas with live preview, 5) Saved simulations tab with 35 existing simulations, 6) Save Simulation and Full Preview buttons.
